entities.description: scent filter based retrieval is a process described as the execution that begins by narrowing the scope
ift is a method or technique with which the execution aligns
selector operators are components that identify relevant  patches  by following  information scents  or applying explicit filter constraints
information scents are described as cues  such as key entities in a question  followed by selector operators
node set n represents the full set of nodes that is reduced by the process
focused node subset ns is the result of the reduction process applied to the full node set n
params sel are parameters used in the selector function to define the focused node subset
patches are relevant units identified by selector operators within the retrieval process
a question is mentioned as a source of key entities used to identify information scents
explicit filter constraints are rules applied by selector operators to identify relevant patches
equation 13 defines the mathematical relationship for the selector function reducing the node set

relations.description: the scent filter based retrieval process aligns with ift
selector operators are the mechanism used within the scent filter based retrieval process to identify relevant patches
selector operators identify relevant patches by following information scents
the process reduces the full node set n to a focused subset
the process results in the creation of the focused node subset ns
selector operators utilize params sel in their function to reduce the node set
information scents include key entities found in a question
selector operators apply explicit filter constraints to identify patches
equation 13 describes the execution of the scent filter based retrieval process
